title: Antherotoma debilis
intro: Antherotoma debilis is a shrub or small tree species native to tropical Africa, commonly known for its medicinal properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, malaria, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimalarial properties. Bioactive constituents of Antherotoma debilis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional African herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into various ointments and creams for topical application.
taxonomy: Antherotoma debilis is classified within the family Melastomataceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome family. This family consists of approximately 165 genera and over 5,000 species. Antherotoma debilis, specifically, is a species of flowering plant that is native to tropical Africa. As a member of the Melastomataceae family, Antherotoma debilis is closely related to other species such as Memecylon and Miconia. The classification of Antherotoma debilis within the Melastomataceae family highlights its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.
